apan recently recounted its islands — and found 7,000 more than anyone realized 🌊.
A new digital survey by the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan identified 14,125 islands, more than double the long-used count of 6,852 from a 1987 Coast Guard report.

The difference isn’t new land. It’s better tech. High-resolution satellite imaging and advanced mapping software can now spot and measure small landmasses that older surveys either missed or lumped together 🛰️.

What once relied on rough estimation is now mapped with precision, revealing thousands of islands that were hidden in plain sight.

READ: A group of retired and active top-ranking officers from the AFP, PNP, PCG, BJMP, and BFP issued a Manifesto of Unwavering Support and Solidarity to the president and commander-in-chief.

In a statement released during its 60th founding anniversary at Camp Aguinaldo and shared by AFP's, the Association of General and Flag Officers (AGFO) strongly condemned "political noise and agitations" calling for the AFP to withdraw support from the President.

AGFO invoked the principle of civilian supremacy over the military and Article XVI, Section 5 of the Constitution, which mandates that the military must remain insulated from partisan politics.

The manifesto also expressed full confidence in the current AFP leadership.

The document was signed by AGFO President and Chairman of the Board, Ret. MGen. Gerardo Layug, along with other officers, trustees, and 75 members. | via Jonathan Magistrado, ABS-CBN News

In a rare and astonishing medical event, a 19-year-old woman from Brazil gave birth to twin boys who each have a different biological father — a phenomenon known as heteropaternal superfecundation. Doctors confirmed through DNA testing that while both babies share the same mother, they were conceived by two different men after the woman reportedly had intercourse with both within the same 24-hour period. Medical experts describe this as an extremely rare occurrence, estimated to happen in only one in a million twin births, highlighting a fascinating and little-known aspect of human reproduction.

#FMTWorld The death toll mounted to over 500 from floods and landslides caused by torrential rains across three countries in Southeast Asia, officials said on Sunday, as relief efforts for tens of thousands of displaced people continued over the weekend.

Malaysia, Indonesia and Thailand faced large-scale devastation after a rare tropical storm formed in the Malacca Strait fuelling heavy rains and wind gusts for a week. There were 336 dead in Indonesia, 170 in Thailand, and two deaths reported in Malaysia.

Rescue and relief officials in the Southeast Asian countries were still trying to get access to many flood-hit areas on Sunday even as flood waters receded and tens of thousands people were evacuated across the three countries.
